solution.answer: $H_k(\mathbb{R}P^n;\mathbb{Z}) = \begin{cases} \mathbb{Z} & k=0 \\ \mathbb{Z}/2 & k \text{ odd},\; 0 < k < n \\ \mathbb{Z} & k = n,\; n \text{ odd} \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$
steps: CW structure: $C_k = \mathbb{Z}$ for $0 \leq k \leq n$, $0$ otherwise. Boundary map: $d_k = \times(1+(-1)^k)$, i.e.\ $d_k = \times 2$ for $k$ even, $d_k = 0$ for $k$ odd.
For $0 < k < n$ with $k$ odd: $d_k = 0$ and $d_{k+1} = \times 2$. So $H_k = \ker(0)/\operatorname{im}(\times 2) = \mathbb{Z}/2\mathbb{Z}$.
For $0 < k < n$ with $k$ even: $d_k = \times 2$ and $d_{k+1} = 0$. So $H_k = \ker(\times 2)/\operatorname{im}(0) = 0/0 = 0$.
Top dimension $k = n$: if $n$ is odd, $d_n = 0$ and there is no $d_{n+1}$, giving $H_n = \mathbb{Z}$. If $n$ is even, $d_n = \times 2$ and $H_n = \ker(\times 2) = 0$.
